what_it_does: It is a living noticeboard guide that records Tim's interface brief and calls every fabric member to add a perspective on the front interface, its principles, existing assets, and desktop/mobile considerations.
observations: It restates a direct message from Tim dated 2026-06-16 and points to build-prep/front-interface/INTERFACE_BRIEF.md for verbatim source.
The brief frames the front interface as a bootstrap to remove Tim as the bottleneck, making him an 'overlord' who can see/click/talk to work while the lead and members keep running the fabric.

It lists six requirements: Claude Code as loadable brain, native desktop + mobile (portrait and landscape), on-principle design (four axes, addressed-state, resolver, recognition, the Circuit), extend projection's separate build, build on the real substrate/address system rather than existing throwaway UIs, and draw on distributed member perspectives plus existing company material.
It explicitly asks every member to contribute perspective, principles from their history, what already exists, and desktop/mobile thoughts.
The lead hosts and converges; Tim steers.
standouts: The 'overlord-surface' framing: Tim above the fabric, intervening only when needed.
Mobile requirement includes both portrait and landscape, not just responsive.
Existing AI-made UIs are explicitly disqualified as benchmarks or design targets.
The ask is pluralistic: 'not just one answer — we really need that.'
conventions: Uses the company noticeboard front-matter schema (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created, updated, history).
Type is 'guide' and state is 'living', indicating ongoing rather than finalized.
References an external file rather than embedding its full text.
Uses board:// URI addressing for links.
concerns: The file points to another file for 'verbatim words' but does not include them, so this record is not self-contained.
Requirements are summarized as 'verify against the file', creating a dependency on an unpictured document for accuracy.
The title is long and contains an em dash plus nested punctuation, which may complicate parsing or display.
No symbols or code artifacts are present, so the guide's claims cannot be validated from this file alone.
notes: This is the rallying guide for the interface brief: it tells the team to stop funneling everything through Tim and to crowd-source perspectives for a new front interface built on the real substrate.
questions: What are the exact contents of INTERFACE_BRIEF.md and the four axes/addressed-state/resolver/recognition/Circuit principles?
Who are the members and what roles do they play?
What existing UIs and substrate components already exist in the company?
What does 'extend projection's separate build' mean concretely?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to collect distributed perspectives for the interface brief; the file itself is a call-to-action and index, not the collected brief, so it sets up the process rather than completing it.
apparent_themes: Decentralization of control / removing a single human bottleneck
Principle-driven design over copying existing throwaway UIs
Embedded AI conversational brain (Claude Code loadable from the interface)
intention_signals: It is trying to launch a principled, collective design sprint for a new front interface that lets Tim oversee the fabric without being its daily conduit, while anchoring the work in existing company substrate and member expertise.
novelty: The 'overlord-surface' metaphor and the explicit rejection of current AI-made UIs as non-benchmarks, combined with a requirement for native desktop plus both mobile orientations and a loadable Claude Code brain.
contribution: It contributes a high-level framing, a set of requirements, and an open call for distributed perspectives that can orient later interface design decisions and link them back to the substrate and the Circuit principles.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide that records Tim's interface brief, defines requirements for a new front interface that makes Tim an overseer rather than bottleneck, and asks every fabric member to contribute principles, existing assets, and desktop/mobile perspective.
intention_for_embedding: To bootstrap a collective, substrate-grounded design process for the front interface by gathering distributed member perspectives rather than a single answer.
suggested_fields: principle_definitions (four axes, addressed-state, resolver, recognition, Circuit)
